title: Is Trump’s Iran War a Crime of Aggression?
content: America’s war on Iran is widely considered an illegal act of aggression in clear violation of the United Nations Charter. But assessing whether the war's planners should face prosecution raises difficult questions, largely owing to ongoing uncertainties about the meaning and scope of criminal aggression.

creator: Lawrence Douglas
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Coming Vote for Europe’s Soul
content: The principle of non-discrimination between trading partners has underpinned international commerce for eight decades. Now, the same principle must guide European lawmakers as they decide whether to ratify the trade agreement reached last year between European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and US President Donald Trump.
snippet: The principle of non-discrimination between trading partners has underpinned international commerce for eight decades. Now, the same principle must guide European lawmakers as they decide whether to r
creator: Kevin Hjortshøj O'Rourke
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Are China’s Young People Fed Up?
content: Decades of misguided economic policymaking have left China in a demographic hole that it seems incapable of climbing out of. As the “world’s factory,” the country is churning out everything except the people whom it will need to sustain its economic development and social stability over the long term.

creator: Yi Fuxian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Myth of the Deserving Billionaire
content: While 55% of Americans say their financial situation is deteriorating, the wealth of the top 1% is reaching record highs. Against this backdrop, defending billionaires from “oppressive” taxation is politically convenient nonsense.
snippet: While 55% of Americans say their financial situation is deteriorating, the wealth of the top 1% is reaching record highs. Against this backdrop, defending billionaires from “oppressive” taxation is po
creator: Teresa Ghilarducci
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Cold Shower for the AI Mania
content: Although generative AI tools have improved rapidly and now outperform humans across many tasks, the market's current euphoria may not be justified. With AI firms increasingly resorting to debt financing, it is worth pausing to consider all the things that could go wrong.
snippet: Although generative AI tools have improved rapidly and now outperform humans across many tasks, the market's current euphoria may not be justified. With AI firms increasingly resorting to debt financi
creator: Raghuram G. Rajan
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Has Taiwan Made Itself Immune to American Betrayal?
content: Although Donald Trump's recent statements about Taiwan seemed like a gift to Chinese President Xi Jinping, such rhetoric no longer tells us as much as it once did. The durability of US-Taiwan relations now rests less on individual leaders than on institutional ties and economic integration, and both have been deepening.
snippet: Although Donald Trump's recent statements about Taiwan seemed like a gift to Chinese President Xi Jinping, such rhetoric no longer tells us as much as it once did. The durability of US-Taiwan relation
creator: Channing Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Trump Sued Himself
content: President Donald Trump has dropped his $10 billion suit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax returns in exchange for a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” to compensate Americans who claim persecution by the previous administration. The point is no longer to conceal corruption but to flaunt impunity.
snippet: President Donald Trump has dropped his $10 billion suit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax returns in exchange for a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” to compensate A
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The US and China Must Learn to Coexist
content: The Beijing summit between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping showed that competition between the United States and China need not become a zero-sum struggle for supremacy. Even as the two countries vie for technological leadership, complete economic decoupling is neither feasible nor desirable.

creator: Pinelopi Koujianou Goldberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Gulf’s Post-OPEC Order
content: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC is the clearest expression yet of its desire to break from the Saudi-led regional order. The result could be a more fragmented Middle East marked by intensifying rivalries among Gulf powers, widening proxy conflicts, and chronic instability.
snippet: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC is the clearest expression yet of its desire to break from the Saudi-led regional order. The result could be a more fragmented Middle East marked by in
creator: Rabah Arezki
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Sustainable Economies Will Own the Future
content: The COVID-19 pandemic and the wars in Ukraine and Iran have reframed the issue of sustainability, which is now as much about sovereignty and economic security as it is about planetary health. Countries and companies that fail to recognize this have everything to lose in the coming years.
snippet: The COVID-19 pandemic and the wars in Ukraine and Iran have reframed the issue of sustainability, which is now as much about sovereignty and economic security as it is about planetary health. Countrie
creator: Bruno Bouygues
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Blueprint for Britain’s Revival
content: There are five big policy-related problems holding back the UK economy. Any politician who tackles them in earnest would benefit from a bond- and equity-market rally, a construction boom, higher business and consumer confidence, and the political dividends that come with broad-based gains to voters' standard of living.
snippet: There are five big policy-related problems holding back the UK economy. Any politician who tackles them in earnest would benefit from a bond- and equity-market rally, a construction boom, higher busin
creator: Jim O'Neill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran Is Pressing Its Strategic Advantage
content: Military and economic coercion usually costs the coercer as well as the target. But by threatening undersea cables beneath the Strait of Hormuz, Iran may have found a way to impose heavy costs on the US government and private sector while generating revenue for itself—at least in the short term.
snippet: Military and economic coercion usually costs the coercer as well as the target. But by threatening undersea cables beneath the Strait of Hormuz, Iran may have found a way to impose heavy costs on the
creator: Carla Norrlöf
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cartographic Justice for Africa
content: For centuries, the Mercator map has distorted Africa’s size, shaping perceptions of the continent's strategic significance and economic prospects. Adopting more accurate map projections is neither technically complex nor prohibitively expensive, yet the symbolic and material consequences could be profound.

creator: Carlos Lopes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From Jefferson to Nehru
content: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an authoritarian resurgence, and the emergence of new forms of domination, measuring up will become only more difficult.
snippet: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an au
creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Brothers in Humiliation
content: Confident in the belief that there is nothing their respective militaries cannot do, Vladimir Putin and Donald Trump led their countries into wars that failed virtually from the start. Now, they are looking to each other for help getting out of the messes they made, but neither holds winning cards.

creator: Carl Bildt
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Africa Is the Key to Sustained Global Growth
content: The rest of the world continues to shunt Africa to the periphery, regarding it mainly as a source of raw materials. But as more advanced economies face structural problems, the continent’s unmatched demographic momentum and enormous industrialization potential imply that it will play a central role in global economic expansion.
snippet: The rest of the world continues to shunt Africa to the periphery, regarding it mainly as a source of raw materials. But as more advanced economies face structural problems, the continent’s unmatched d
creator: Hippolyte Fofack
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Root of Today’s Global Imbalances
content: Today’s global imbalances are driven primarily by domestic saving-investment dynamics and the interaction between geopolitical rivalry, technological competition, and capital flows. Only by taking coordinated action to manage the associated risks can the world prevent today’s tensions from erupting in another global economic crisis.

creator: Lee Jong-Wha
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: When Volatility Is Good Politics
content: The Iran war has revealed how deeply material interests are embedded in strategic calculations, concentrating rewards among powerful insiders while leaving society to absorb the costs. As long as chaos remains politically and economically rewarding, such conflicts will remain difficult to contain.
